blockers.description: Plan 05.1-01 audio-resume fix does NOT actually resume audio on real iPhone iOS Safari. Symptoms: <30s lock → session continues running, AC apparently dead, mute/unmute toggle doesn't restore audio, only starting a NEW session restores audio. ≥30s lock → iOS discards the tab entirely (full reload to setup screen — out of app's reach). The Plan 01 spec assumed iOS only SUSPENDS the AudioContext on lock; empirical behavior is that iOS likely CLOSES the AC, so audioCtx.resume() rejects (silently absorbed per D-09) and there's no recovery path without reconstructing the engine.
blockers.workaround: Diagnose first via Mac+iPhone Safari Web Inspector to confirm audioCtx.state ('suspended' vs 'closed') after unlock + whether visibilitychange→visible fires + whether engine.resume() is called and resolves vs rejects.

blockers.description: iOS Safari ≥30s background discard. Tab is killed under memory pressure; React/JS state is lost. Plan 01 cannot help here. Phase 4 persistence restores user-facing settings, which is the maximum recovery possible without redesigning around server-side session state. Document as known iOS limitation; not a Plan 01 bug.
blockers.workaround: None at app level. Document in UAT log.

blockers.description: Firefox Desktop scale-animation flicker (FF-01) — slight residual flicker during orb scaling. Mitigation stack applied (will-change + translate3d) reduced but did not eliminate. Kitchen-sink (scale3d + backface-visibility) was reverted at user request. Phase 5.1 success criterion #2 names Safari only; Firefox is not a primary target. Acceptable as carry-forward to v1.x or Phase 6.
blockers.workaround: Logged in 05.1-UAT.md Task 3 Caveats section. Real fix likely requires CSS-keyframe-driven animation instead of per-frame React inline transform writes — out of Phase 5.1 budget.

human_actions_pending.action: User runs Mac+iPhone Safari Web Inspector diagnostic on the audio resume bug. Connect iPhone to Mac via USB; Safari menu → Develop → [iPhone] → inspect the running tab. Capture: (1) audioCtx.state value after unlock, (2) whether the 'visibilitychange' event fires on unlock (add console.log in onVisibility handler temporarily, OR set a breakpoint), (3) whether engine.resume() resolves or rejects, (4) any console errors during lock/unlock cycle.
human_actions_pending.context: Determines which of three root causes is real: (a) AC closed not suspended → need full engine reconstruction, (b) listener not firing → need different event hook, (c) resume() succeeds but cue scheduler events stale → need re-anchoring on resume. Choice between Plan 05.1-06 designs depends on this answer.

human_actions_pending.action: User runs UAT Task 4 (Phase 1-5 regression sweep) on real Safari (desktop OR iOS) after Task 2 audio-resume bug is resolved. Skip the wake-lock part on iPhone-over-HTTP; use macOS Safari over localhost for wake-lock check. Record per-phase PASS/FAIL with notes in 05.1-UAT.md Task 4 block.
human_actions_pending.context: Phase 5.1 success criterion #3: Phase 1-5 must remain green. Auto suite (Task 1) is PASS; manual sweep on real Safari is the human-verification half.

human_actions_pending.action: User fills Sign-off block in 05.1-UAT.md and types 'approved' (or describes failure mode for plan-checker escalation).
human_actions_pending.context: Phase 5.1 closes only when sign-off is written.

decisions.decision: Phase 5.1 was inserted AFTER Phase 5 closed, to hold the carry-forward defects from 05-04-UAT-LOG.md Findings 1+2 (orb visual gap + iOS audio resume).
decisions.rationale: Surgical scope; doesn't reopen Phase 5 milestone.

decisions.decision: Plan 05.1-04 was inserted AFTER Task 3 of Plan 03 UAT FAILED on Safari Desktop. Plan 02's 1.5px border fix was insufficient — root cause was deeper grid+abs-pos sizing collapse.
decisions.rationale: Web Inspector data showed .orb width = 209px (58% of 360 container) due to WR-03 quirk on absolutely-positioned grid children with inset-0 on Safari Desktop.

decisions.decision: Plan 04 final form: 4-edge anchoring (left:0/right:0/top:0/bottom:0, NOT width:100%/height:100%) on .orb + drop the .orb {transition: transform 200ms} + will-change:transform + inline translate3d(0,0,0) scale(s). 5 commits of iteration.
decisions.rationale: Initial Plan 04 used width:100%/height:100% which sized correctly but FROZE Safari's transform transition (computed stuck at scale(0.58) despite inline scale(0.99) — Safari restarts transition on every inline-style reassignment when sizing properties are present). Removing transition + 4-edge anchoring fixed Safari/iOS/Chrome. Firefox needed GPU-promotion hints (will-change + translate3d combo) to suppress flicker; kitchen-sink scale3d + backface-visibility was reverted as marginal.

decisions.decision: Plan 05.1-05 (Issue B inner-ring UX symmetry — out-phase has no 'approaching boundary' cue) is DEFERRED until Plan 5.1-03 closes and Plan 01 audio bug is resolved.
decisions.rationale: Out-of-scope for Plan 02 per D-11 (inner ring untouched). User chose to expand Phase 5.1 to include it via separate discuss→plan→execute cycle. Sequence: fix audio bug → close UAT → ship 05.1-04 visual fixes (already shipped) → spec/plan/execute 05.1-05.

next_action: User runs Mac+iPhone Safari Web Inspector diagnostic on the audio-resume bug (see human_actions_pending #1). Goal: determine audioCtx.state after iOS Safari unlock — is it 'suspended' (resume() should work; bug elsewhere) or 'closed' (need engine reconstruction)? Once answered, agent specs Plan 05.1-06 to fix the audio bug accordingly, OR scopes the bug as carry-forward to v1.x. After audio fix lands, user runs Task 4 regression sweep + writes sign-off block. Then agent creates 05.1-03-SUMMARY, closes Phase 5.1, and routes to Plan 05.1-05 (Issue B).
context_notes: Phase 5.1 was scoped as a surgical 2-3 plan polish phase; it has now consumed 4 plans + 8+ commits of post-UAT iteration on Plan 04 + an uncovered Plan 01 spec inadequacy. The scope-creep path was driven entirely by real-device UAT findings, all of which were correct calls — no rework was wasted. The orb visual fix is empirically solid across 4 browsers (Firefox flicker is a known caveat). The audio fix is the remaining blocker. Plan 01 code matches its spec verbatim; the spec itself underestimated iOS Safari's auto-close behavior on lock. Diagnosis-then-decide is the right next move (vs preemptively shipping Plan 05.1-06).
